impact: The daemon has now hit the same syntactic failure pattern on both source freshness and missing-information validation. This repair narrows task selection, makes the active broad validator non-selectable, and gives the worker deterministic recovery tasks that can pass one daemon cycle at a time before returning to implementation work.
summary: Park the newly failing broad missing-information validator task and extend supervisor recovery guidance so the daemon resumes with syntax-first, fixture-first repair tasks instead of retrying invalid Python proposals.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 2 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-54: Add a source freshness fixture that classifies stale PP&D guidance, changed content hashes, removed pages, and recrawl priority without performing a live recrawl.; Task checkbox-55: Add a fixture-only change report renderer for public PP&D source freshness results that summarizes changed requirements, new or removed links, and affected permit-process fixtures with citation references.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
